text: Variables should use camelCase naming convention.
Class names should use PascalCase. Example: UserAccount, OrderService.
Function names should be descriptive and indicate action. Avoid vague names like doStuff or handleIt.
Constants should be written in uppercase with underscores. Example: MAX_RETRIES, API_URL.
Functions should include try-catch blocks for error handling.
Avoid empty catch blocks. Always log or handle errors appropriately.
Return meaningful error messages instead of generic ones like 'Something went wrong'.
Validate inputs before processing to reduce runtime errors.
API keys should never be exposed in frontend code.
Always validate and sanitize user inputs to prevent injection attacks.
Use HTTPS for all API requests to ensure secure data transmission.
Do not store passwords in plain text. Always hash passwords using a secure algorithm.
Avoid unnecessary loops and redundant computations to improve performance.
Use efficient data structures such as maps or sets when appropriate.
